item.title: Only 6,679 more bottom shelf memes to go
item.description:
❤️
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
Join for the games. Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving degen there to play it with you. Join for the memes. We share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the NSFW memes. Join to ping @humlyhums Join to meet @crimebrulee the biggest problem in the server. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
Join for the meme competitions
Now only 6,679 more memes!
Deaf cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax You can catch her live on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka

item.title: Getting a lot better
item.description: Two and a half years ago I was in a car and acquired a brain injury. They said I might not walk again I spent months in a wheelchair. Now I'm almost running again! #inspirational #traumaticbraininjury #disabled

item.title: Sfincione (Sicilian Pizza)
item.description: Sfincione (Sicilian Pizza) Ingredients - 500g (4 cups) bread flour - 500g (4 cups) durum wheat semolina - 14g (1 tbsp) dry instant yeast - 100ml (7 tbsp) extra-virgin olive oil - 12g (2 tsp) salt - 2 yellow onions, thinly sliced - 10 anchovies - 2 cans (400g each) peeled tomatoes - 200g (7 oz) provolone cheese, diced - 40g (1/3 cup) breadcrumbs - 1 tsp dried oregano - 40g (1/3 cup) finely grated pecorino cheese Method 1. In a stand mixer with a dough hook attachment, combine the bread flour, durum wheat semolina, yeast, and salt. Set to medium-low speed. Once mixed, gradually add 700ml (3 cups) of warm water, mixing for 2-3 minutes until fully absorbed. Add the olive oil and continue mixing for another 3-4 minutes until the dough is smooth. Note that this will be a high-hydration dough, so it will be quite sticky. 2. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 1-2 minutes until a smooth, tight ball forms. Transfer it to a clean, oiled bowl, cover with a tea towel, and let it prove for about 1 hour or until it has doubled in size. 3. Once the dough has risen, generously brush a full-size sheet pan with olive oil. Place the dough in the pan and gently stretch it to fill the entire surface. Cover again with a tea towel and let it prove for another hour or until doubled in size. 4. While the dough is proving, preheat the oven to 240°C/475°F (fan-forced). 5. In a pan over medium heat, warm 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Add the sliced onions, 4 anchovies, and a pinch of salt. Sauté for 6-7 minutes, stirring often, until the onions are softened and lightly golden. 6. Add the peeled tomatoes to the pan, then add one-third of a tin of water to each empty can, swish, and pour into the pan. Break up the tomatoes and cook over medium-low heat for about 1 hour, stirring occasionally. 7. In a small bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, dried oregano, and grated pecorino cheese. Set aside. 8. Once the dough has risen a second time, remove the tea towel. Drizzle a bit of olive oil over the surface, then evenly press diced pieces of provolone cheese into the dough. Place one-third of an anchovy near each cheese piece, pressing them into the dough. 9. Place the dough in the preheated oven and bake for 12 minutes, until the base is nearly cooked through. 10. Remove the pizza from the oven and spread the prepared sauce evenly over the top. Sprinkle with the breadcrumb, oregano, and pecorino mixture. Return to the oven and bake for an additional 6-7 minutes, until the sauce bubbles and the top is golden. 11. Remove from the oven and let the sfincione rest for 5 minutes before carefully removing it from the pan. Let it cool slightly, then cut and serve warm.
item.dc:creator: skibbyAU

item.dc:creator: pgkobrien

item.title: I might be in trouble…
item.description:
I’ve not been feeling well since the end of September. I had a bad cold, that turned into a sinus infection and then upper respiratory infection by mid-October. The URI cleared up for the most part, but it appeared to be lingering just enough to make it hard to breathe. Without insurance and very little work recently, I didn’t have the money to go to the E.R. and my new doctor couldn’t schedule me for my first appointment until mid-November. So, I told myself that I just needed to tough it out for a few more weeks and I’d be able to get some antibiotics to evict this infection. I made it to my doctor appoint on Thursday morning, the doctor took one look at me & my list of symptoms and told me to go straight to the E.R. He called ahead for me and 20 minutes later I was in a room in the E.R. with tests being run. The last two and a half days have been stressful af! Initial monitoring indicated very high blood pressure and liquid in & around my lungs and heart. An ultrasound of my heart and kidneys showed reduced functionality in both, with my heart being weak flow and my kidneys struggling. They put me on diuretics and BP medication. My BP was north of 200/100 and I’d put on more than 30 pounds in the last 3 weeks. They’ve run me through a huge number of tests over the last several days, meanwhile I’ve slept. I’ve been so exhausted and stressed out that I’ve either been sleeping or in the bathroom for most of the last 60+ hours. They’ve been changing my medication around trying to balance this failing house of cards, there’s my heart, fluid retention from my feet up to my mid chest, and now I’ve found out that my kidneys are functioning at 20% with as much as 10 grams of protein leaking into my urine!!! With the kidneys unable to take the stress of my massive fluid dump (3+ liters yesterday alone), they’ve decided to take me off the diuretics for now to give the twins a break, but the protein leakage is off the charts and no amount of BP medication seems to be able to get me below 167/77. The doctors have been punting the problem back and forth between departments, with no one wanting to take responsibility for my case. They stop by, say high, point fingers at another department and then leave. Today that changed, nephrology stopped by and for the first time a doctor sat down in my room and spent half an hour talking to me about my case and what she sees in my labs. She wanted to know what I’m eating at home, how my cold progressed and actually took the time to dig into my medical history for clues!
Nephrology believes that the sinus infection that I had in early October moved either to my heart or my kidneys. This caused fluid retention, causing the shortness of breath and high BP. They’ve taken more blood for lab tests about two hours ago and we’ll know more over the coming days, but nephrology doesn’t think I can get out of here until Tuesday or Wednesday at the earliest. This has already been a hard year, some ups, but a lot of downs (job loss, no insurance, ex-wife, etc.) and this just adds to it! I’m not scared for me, but I’m responsible for three little girls as their mom can barely take care of herself most days. I have a wonderful girlfriend who is amazing and has taken over all the house and most of the girl’s responsibilities. I’m scared for my girls, I need to be there for them to make sure they have an amazing childhood, a stable home, and are taught how to love and respect all people (something they won’t get from almost anyone else that might end up raising them)! Not much to do now but wait and scream into the void! Thanks for listening to me ramble and for keeping me laughing instead of crying throughout this.
item.dc:creator: Ranlothir

item.title: I think this is it. Locking in
item.description: I also almost ruined the first date because she thought I was taking pics of her butt. I walked her to her car and it started pouring rain so she offered to drive me to mine. She bent over and started clearing her passenger seat. At the same time my sister sent my a bunch of texts. She normally doesn’t text like that so I checked them. However, the angle of my phone made it look like I was taking pics. She looked back super freaked out. Luckily I reacted properly and she still went on a second date
item.dc:creator: knightsLikeThisOnANightLikeThis
